source_paper_title: Nash Equilibria for Dividend Distribution with Competition
context:
Section 4 (Asymmetric initial endowment), Remark 4.9, page 23 (PDF page numbering in the provided text)

The question as to whether an equilibrium exists in which Player 1 uses a strategy and Player 2 uses a control (both either pure or randomised) is open when \(y>x\), especially if we seek an equilibrium valid for all parameter choices and initial states. We leave the question open for future research.
title: Existence of a Nash equilibrium with the rich firm using a (possibly randomised) feedback strategy and the poor firm using a control in the asymmetric-endowment dividend game
latex:
Consider the following two-player continuous-time stochastic game.

- Fix parameters \(\mu_0>0\), \(\hat\mu>\mu_0\), \(\sigma>0\), and \(r>0\).
- Let \((B_t)_{t\ge 0}\) be a standard one-dimensional Brownian motion on a filtered probability space \(((\Omega,\mathcal F,(\mathcal F_t)_{t\ge0}),\mathbb P)\).
- Let initial cash reserves satisfy \(0\le x<y\). 
- Player 1 (the poorer firm) chooses a dividend cumulative process \(L=(L_t)_{t\ge0}\) and Player 2 (the richer firm) chooses \(D=(D_t)_{t\ge0}\). An admissible control is any right-continuous, nondecreasing, \((\mathcal F_t)\)-adapted process with \(L_{0-}=0\) (resp. \(D_{0-}=0\)) and jump constraint
\[
\Delta L_t \le (X_{t-})_+ ,\qquad \Delta D_t \le (Y_{t-})_+ ,\qquad \forall t\ge 0,
\]
where \((u)_+:=\max\{u,0\}\), and the controlled cash reserves are
\[
X_t = x + \mu_0 t + \sigma B_t - L_t,\qquad Y_t = y + \mu_0 t + \sigma B_t - D_t.
\]
- Default times are \(\gamma_X:=\inf\{t\ge0: X_t\le 0\}\) and \(\gamma_Y:=\inf\{t\ge0: Y_t\le 0\}\).
- If one firm defaults first, the surviving firm becomes a monopolist and receives continuation value \(\hat v(\cdot)\), the value function of the single-firm dividend problem with drift \(\hat\mu\):
\[
\hat v(z):=\sup_{\xi}\,\mathbb E\Big[\int_{[0,\gamma_C]} e^{-rt}\, d\xi_t\Big],\quad C_t=z+\hat\mu t+\sigma B_t-\xi_t,\quad \gamma_C:=\inf\{t\ge0: C_t\le 0\},
\]
with the supremum over admissible dividend controls \(\xi\).
- Given \((L,D)\), the payoffs are
\[
J_1^{x,y}(L,D)=\mathbb E\Big[\int_{[0,\gamma_X\wedge\gamma_Y]} e^{-rt}\, dL_t + \mathbf 1_{\{\gamma_Y<\gamma_X\}}e^{-r\gamma_Y}\hat v(X_{\gamma_Y})\Big],
\]
\[
J_2^{x,y}(D,L)=\mathbb E\Big[\int_{[0,\gamma_X\wedge\gamma_Y]} e^{-rt}\, dD_t + \mathbf 1_{\{\gamma_X<\gamma_Y\}}e^{-r\gamma_X}\hat v(Y_{\gamma_X})\Big].
\]

A (pure) feedback strategy for Player \(i\in\{1,2\}\) is a non-anticipative map that assigns a dividend path to the player as a functional of the observed Brownian path and the opponent’s past dividend path; a randomised strategy additionally depends on an auxiliary independent randomisation variable \(u\in[0,1]\), as in Definition 3.1 of the paper. A pure control is a strategy that depends only on the Brownian path.

Problem: Determine whether, for every choice of parameters \((\mu_0,\hat\mu,\sigma,r)\) and every \((x,y)\) with \(0\le x<y\), there exists a Nash equilibrium \((\Xi_1^*,\Xi_2^*)\) (in the sense of Definition 3.6 of the paper, allowing randomised strategies) such that:

- Player 1 (the poorer firm) uses an admissible control (i.e., \(\Xi_1^*\) is a pure control, depending only on \((B_t)\)), and
- Player 2 (the richer firm) uses a feedback strategy (pure or randomised) that may depend on Player 1’s observed dividend path (i.e., \(\Xi_2^*\) is a strategy in the sense of Definition 3.1, not necessarily reducible to a control).

Equivalently, decide whether there exists an equilibrium of the asymmetric-endowment game with roles reversed relative to Theorem 4.1, namely an equilibrium of “strategy vs. control” type where the richer firm plays a (possibly randomised) feedback strategy and the poorer firm plays a control, valid uniformly over all parameter choices and initial states \(0\le x<y\).
